messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "europe-30pct-arithmetic-ghost",
 "title": "The 'Europe ≈ 30%' hop is an inserted number; two sourced regional shares imply Europe is ≤24%",
 "narrative": "The single most load-bearing hop in tamBase — Europe's share of the global rack-PDU market — is stated by no source. Grand View publishes only NA = 38%; MarketIntelo puts Asia-Pacific at 38.2% and calls it the DOMINANT region. If both regional leaders hold ~38%, Europe plus rest-of-world share a ~24% residual, and Europe alone plausibly sits at 20–23%, not 30%. The 30% was inferred from NA's share alone, before the APAC datapoint existed in the corpus. A 7-point regional-share error moves the base ~23% by itself, and unlike the tornado's band it is a directional bias, not symmetric noise.",
 "category": "fact",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "No retrieved source states a Europe share of the global rack-PDU market; the chain inserts ≈30% by analogy to Grand View's NA = 38%",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-base#c1",
    "ledger:tamBase"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "MarketIntelo independently reports Asia-Pacific dominated the rack-PDU market with 38.2% revenue share; NA 38% + APAC 38.2% leaves ≤23.8% for Europe, MEA and LatAm combined, capping Europe well below the inserted 30%",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c7",
    "digest:tam-global-crosscheck#c1"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "tamBase is linear in the Europe-share hop: 30%→23% cuts the CE base ~23%, compounding with (not covered by) the ±20% band, and dragging SAM and YAM down proportionally",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The expression reads like sourced arithmetic — '$2.81B × Europe ≈ 30% [est.; NA = 38% per same source]' — so a reviewer sees a citation next to the hop and assumes the 30% is anchored. The bracketed 'est.' is the only tell that the decisive multiplier is invented, and the corroborating APAC datapoint lives in a different publisher's report the model never joined against.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "A published regional revenue table for the rack-PDU category (Grand View regional chapter, IndexBox world-vs-EU headline pair, or Omdia) showing Europe at ≥28% of global revenue.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "Two independent sources sum NA+APAC to ~76%, directly constraining Europe below 30%; the counterweight is that MarketIntelo's category scope may differ from Grand View's, so the two shares may not be strictly additive",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.77,
   "note": "Europe share corrected from 30% to ~23% residual; all other hops held constant"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Europe regional revenue share in any rack-PDU-specific report vintage",
   "where": "Grand View rack-PDU report regional segmentation; IndexBox world benchmark report vs EU report headline ratio",
   "threshold": "Europe stated or derivable at <27%",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "EMEA vs Americas vs APAC rack-power revenue mix in incumbent disclosures",
   "where": "Vertiv 10-K segment reporting; Legrand annual report data-center line commentary",
   "threshold": "EMEA rack-power consistently <25% of global segment revenue",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Purchase the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report and the world benchmark report together to compute Europe's share directly instead of inferring it",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "This is the exact hop the ledger's own promotionPath targets; the affordance table already prices the information at €30M of TAM swing"
  },
  {
   "action": "Rebuild tamBase bottom-up (CE MW × €-per-MW PDU intensity from vendor fit-out BOMs) so the regional-share hop is bypassed entirely",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "A second independent method converts the untestable 30% into a cross-checkable intensity assumption"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "definition-scopedown",
 "riskId": "risk.definition-scopedown.europe-30pct-arithmetic-ghost"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

Grand View publishes only NA = 38%; MarketIntelo puts Asia-Pacific at 38.2% and calls it the DOMINANT region. all other hops held constant"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Europe regional revenue share in any rack-PDU-specific report vintage","where":"Grand View rack-PDU report regional segmentation; IndexBox world benchmark report vs EU report headline ratio","threshold":"Europe stated or derivable at <27%","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"EMEA vs Americas vs APAC rack-power revenue mix in incumbent disclosures","where":"Vertiv 10-K segment reporting; Legrand annual report data-center line commentary","threshold":"EMEA rack-power consistently <25% of global segment revenue","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Purchase the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report and the world benchmark report together to compute Europe's share directly instead of inferring it","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"This is the exact hop the ledger's own promotionPath targets; the affordance table already prices the information at €30M of TAM swing"},{"action":"Rebuild tamBase bottom-up (CE MW × €-per-MW PDU intensity from vendor fit-out BOMs) so the regional-share hop is bypassed entirely","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"A second independent method converts the untestable 30% into a cross-checkable intensity assumption"}]}
